mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of expected equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than expected, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold crossing shows no predictive power over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in forward 60-day returns during periods where both proxies rise simultaneously, or cross-validation IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, real consumer credit growth becomes the primary driver of equity valuations; accelerating credit expansion sustains consumer spending and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns despite higher discount rates.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns turn negative when RSAFS growth decelerates during rate hikes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in post-2008 data windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical 2.5% annualized trend indicates robust aggregate demand, which directly lifts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when GDPC1 growth exceeds trend, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.04 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the industrial production index below 105 signals weakening manufacturing demand and resolving supply chain bottlenecks, which compresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the threshold crossing shows no statistical edge over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with declining unemployment creates a wage-price spiral risk, prompting aggressive monetary tightening expectations that discount future earnings and yield negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both PAYEMS growth and UNRATE declines occur simultaneously, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-factor models.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, housing starts act as a leading indicator of consumer confidence and durable goods demand; accelerating housing construction supports positive S&P 500 forward returns as it signals resilient real economic activity.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when housing starts decline during high inflation, or the conditional signal fails to beat a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.
